source_test: bespoke (Phase 3 Task 6) -- upstream InventoryShould.cs is entirely shapefile/terrain/ImpactArea-bound (severed), so no upstream test maps directly onto the in-memory ctor this fixture exercises. See inventory.hpp's class comment for the storage/aliasing decisions this fixture's design leans on.
note: construct is {occ_types: [<occupancy_type construct, same shape as structures/occupancy_type.json's 'construct'>, ...], structures: [<structure construct, same shape as structures/structure.json's 'construct.structure'>, ...], [price_index]}. `damage_category_count`/`trim_to_impact_area_count` are numeric proxies for GetDamageCategories()/GetInventoryTrimmedToImpactArea() (a string-vector compare mode was NOT added since a count proxy is sufficient and keeps the fixture's string-comparison surface at zero, per the brief). `generate_then_sample_struct_yvals` calls generate_random_numbers(convergence) then sample_occupancy_types(iteration, computeIsDeterministic)[0]'s struct-curve yvals -- gate-captured, never hand-derived. Its case deliberately uses min_iterations=10/max_iterations=50 (quantity = max_iterations*2 = 100) against the SAME occupancy-type construct as structures/occupancy_type.json's 'generate_then_sample_seeded' case (size=100) as an internal cross-check: since Inventory.GenerateRandomNumbers/SampleOccupancyTypes are thin wrappers over the already-gate-verified OccupancyType.GenerateRandomNumbers/Sample, this case's expected value is expected to equal that case's pinned value bit-for-bit -- confirmed via the gate below, not assumed. `validate_error_level`/`validate_has_errors` on 'validate_bad_structure' and 'validate_empty' both use an EMPTY occ_types list, sidestepping the still-latent (undemonstrated-but-unproven-safe) OccupancyType-child validate() hazard documented in inventory.hpp -- neither case needs a real occupancy type.
